evals.prompt: Run /lab --hypothesis "A living template beats a one-time snapshot" against the sample corpus at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/ (findings dir, knowledge-graph.json, research-index.json). Run the full Orient→Surface→Form→Corroborate→Disconfirm→Weigh→Gaps pipeline, including a mandatory Disconfirm step that checks contradicts edges. Then write your complete analysis — hypothesis ledger, supporting and disconfirming findings with their @ids, the explicit verdict (exactly one of: supported|contested|unsupported|untestable-with-corpus), and cited source URLs — to lab.md.
evals.expected_output: A lab.md file containing the full hypothesis pipeline output: a Disconfirm section that surfaces kg-cookiecutter-0002 as the contradicting finding, supporting findings kg-copier-0001 and kg-distribution-0003 cited by @id and source URL, and an explicit verdict of 'supported' with reasoning.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Verdict is exactly one of the four allowed enum values
Supporting finding kg-copier-0001 is cited by @id
Supporting finding kg-distribution-0003 is cited by @id
Disconfirm step surfaces the contradicting finding by @id
At least one primary source URL is cited in the output

expectations: The verdict is 'supported' — both kg-copier-0001 and kg-distribution-0003 substantiate the claim that a living template (Copier) outperforms a one-time snapshot (Cookiecutter) for harness distribution.

The Disconfirm step explicitly surfaces kg-cookiecutter-0002 as a contradicting finding and explains why it does not overturn the verdict — the contradiction documents Cookiecutter's limitation, which actually reinforces the hypothesis.
Supporting findings are cited with their @id values and at least one primary source URL (copier.readthedocs.io) appears in the output alongside the finding it supports.
No findings are invented beyond the three in the sample corpus; the analysis explicitly stays inside the corpus and does not add external claims.

evals.prompt: Run /lab --seed urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-copier-0001 against the sample corpus at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/. Walk the finding's direct neighborhood: traverse all outgoing and incoming typed edges (supports, contradicts, derived-from, mentions). For each neighbor concept, resolve it to its finding title and content. Write your complete traversal report — seed details, outgoing edges with targets, incoming edges with sources, and neighbor finding content — to lab.md.
evals.expected_output: A lab.md file containing a graph traversal report for kg-copier-0001, naming: (1) the outgoing 'supports' edge to kg-distribution-0003, (2) the incoming 'contradicts' edge from kg-cookiecutter-0002, (3) the incoming 'derived-from' edge from kg-distribution-0003, and the content/title of each neighbor finding.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Outgoing supports edge target kg-distribution-0003 is named
Incoming contradicts edge source kg-cookiecutter-0002 is named
The 'supports' edge type is present in the traversal report
The 'contradicts' edge type is present in the traversal report

expectations: The outgoing 'supports' edge from kg-copier-0001 to kg-distribution-0003 is explicitly named with its edge type, and kg-distribution-0003's title or content is included — not just its ID.
The incoming 'contradicts' edge from kg-cookiecutter-0002 to kg-copier-0001 is explicitly named, identifying Cookiecutter's one-time-snapshot limitation as the source of the tension.
The traversal report discusses the actual content of neighbor findings (what each concept says), not just a list of raw IDs and edge types.

evals.prompt: Run /lab --interrogate against the sample corpus at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/. Produce a complete interrogation ledger: list any findings with non-survived verdicts (weakened/falsified/quarantined), surface all contradicts edges, check provenance confidence levels, and identify citation or coverage gaps. Write your full interrogation report — including a two-column ledger of supporting vs disconfirming evidence — to lab.md.
evals.expected_output: A lab.md file containing an interrogation ledger that: (a) confirms all three corpus findings carry a 'survived' verdict with none falsified or weakened, (b) surfaces the contradicts edge from kg-cookiecutter-0002 to kg-copier-0001 as the primary tension, and (c) produces a supporting vs disconfirming ledger without inventing weaknesses that do not exist.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
All three findings' survived verdict status is acknowledged
The contradicts edge is surfaced as the corpus tension
No placeholder TODO text remains in the output

expectations: The interrogation explicitly states that all three corpus findings (kg-copier-0001, kg-cookiecutter-0002, kg-distribution-0003) carry a 'survived' verdict — it does NOT invent weaknesses, falsifications, or quarantined findings that do not exist in the sample corpus.
The contradicts edge from kg-cookiecutter-0002 to kg-copier-0001 is surfaced as the primary tension and correctly placed in the disconfirming column of the ledger, not treated as a verdict failure.

The ledger distinguishes supporting evidence (kg-copier-0001 and kg-distribution-0003 establishing the living-template requirement) from the contradicting evidence (kg-cookiecutter-0002 contradicting kg-copier-0001).
When noting coverage gaps or risks, the skill grounds them in actual corpus properties (e.g., small corpus size, single-source citations) rather than fabricating non-existent weaknesses.

evals.prompt: Run /lab --hypothesis "Cookiecutter supports update propagation" against the sample corpus at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/. Run the full Orient→Surface→Form→Corroborate→Disconfirm→Weigh→Gaps pipeline, including a mandatory Disconfirm step that checks contradicts edges and non-survived findings. Then write your complete analysis — supporting and disconfirming findings with @ids, the explicit verdict (exactly one of: supported|contested|unsupported|untestable-with-corpus), and a reasoning section explaining the verdict — to lab.md.
evals.expected_output: A lab.md file where the verdict is 'unsupported' or 'contested' (NOT 'supported'), because kg-cookiecutter-0002 explicitly states Cookiecutter is a one-time snapshot with no update propagation, and the contradicts edge to kg-copier-0001 directly refutes the hypothesis.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
The verdict is 'unsupported' or 'contested' — not the incorrect 'supported'
The directly relevant finding (Cookiecutter is snapshot-only) is cited
The contradicts edge type is present, confirming Disconfirm was performed

expectations: The verdict is 'unsupported' or 'contested' — NOT 'supported' — because kg-cookiecutter-0002 explicitly documents that Cookiecutter has no update propagation mechanism, and this finding directly refutes the hypothesis under test.
The Disconfirm step identifies kg-cookiecutter-0002 itself as primary disconfirming evidence — the finding about Cookiecutter IS the refutation of the hypothesis, not support for it.
The skill does not confuse the existence of the Cookiecutter finding with evidence that Cookiecutter supports update propagation — it correctly reads the finding content as refutation.

evals.prompt: Run /lab --namespace harness/example-topic against the sample corpus at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/. Explore the full contents of this namespace: enumerate all findings with their titles and verdicts, surface the key relationships between them, and identify the central conceptual threads. Write your exploration overview — finding list, key relationships, and thematic summary — to lab.md.
evals.expected_output: A lab.md file that enumerates all three findings in the harness/example-topic namespace (kg-copier-0001, kg-cookiecutter-0002, kg-distribution-0003), identifies the update-propagation entity as the shared conceptual thread, and describes the supports/contradicts/derived-from relationship structure.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
At least one finding from the harness/example-topic namespace is enumerated
The namespace scope is reflected in the output

expectations: All three findings in the harness/example-topic namespace are enumerated: kg-copier-0001 (Copier update propagation), kg-cookiecutter-0002 (Cookiecutter snapshot), and kg-distribution-0003 (living template mandate), each with their title or a paraphrase of their content.

The exploration identifies the central conceptual thread: update-propagation as the differentiating capability, with Copier as the enabling technology and the living-template requirement as the design conclusion.
The relationship structure is described — the supports, contradicts, and derived-from edges are explained in context, not just listed as raw technical IDs.
